rationale: The sole ground-truth concern (yJader) is that both submit_initial and submit_update already reject list prompts, so gating the unwrap on already_submitted was inconsistent — X independently verifies this exact fact by citing both raise sites (stage_pool.py:951, 1022) and concludes the fix is correct, matching the reviewer's actual reasoning; it also correctly validates the DiffusionOutput change and lands on APPROVE, matching the real outcome (two LGTMs). Y touches the same line but hedges into an unverified, more speculative worry ('if the processor expects a list, the guard must be restored') without doing the confirming investigation, pads the review with duplicate comments on the same line, and cites a specific issue number/repro command (#4839) not grounded in the provided diff or ground truth, undermining precision; its COMMENT verdict also diverges from the actual approved outcome.
